summary:
EVERYDAY LIFE AND SPIRITUAL TEACHINGS: Brandon recounts visiting the Oklahoma City Zoo Lights with his family, noting the expense but appreciating the experience. He then transitions to sharing spiritual lessons he received about the importance of maintaining a love walk, especially when faced with offense or betrayal. Brandon states that the Lord has emphasized 2025 as a year of warning and 2026 as a time when believers will be drawn closer, leading to significant shakings and awakenings. He recounts visitations from angels, including one wearing opal armor, who delivered a message that walking in love is the key to experiencing a greater glory. Brandon emphasizes the necessity of staying vertical by focusing on God rather than horizontal distractions, and he recounts being instructed to keep his inner circle small and to write down his visions for 2026.

PAST EXPERIENCES AND HUMILITY: Brandon shares memories of his past struggles with reading and the shame it caused him during youth gatherings. He details his time working as a janitor, where he cleaned toilets and felt ignored by others. During this period, Brandon states he prayed for a young boy with stage four facial cancer while cleaning a church, and the boy was completely healed three days later. Brandon notes that God used these humble beginnings to prepare him for his current ministry. He mentions declining a recent invitation to a White House Christmas party attended by President Trump and other prominent figures, choosing instead to remain small in his own eyes and maintain his current lifestyle. Brandon also discusses the potential of opening a physical church near Oral Roberts University in Tulsa, though he acknowledges the need for extensive armed security due to opposition he faces.

PROPHETIC VISIONS OF INDIVIDUALS AND NATIONS: Brandon describes a vision of a highly structured woman with braided hair who is carrying bitterness from two previous divorces into a new marriage planned for the spring of 2026, warning her to release the past to save her future. Shifting to international and political visions, Brandon recounts seeing an escalation in the Caribbean involving Soldado Bay, where oil wells and rigs are set on fire by Maduro, producing thick black smoke that reaches the United States, reminiscent of Saddam Hussein's tactics. He also describes a vision of a demonic altar featuring an upside-down cross and a picture of Donald Trump, prompting him to declare protection over the former president and his family. Brandon details seeing Trump on a golf course and a crystal ball with a castle, which he associates with financial attacks.

ECONOMIC AND POLITICAL FORECASTS: Brandon shares a vision of a massive, spotted black python wrapping around the United States from Louisiana to Canada, attempting to suffocate the nation's economy. He describes seeing the Iraqi dinar, specifically a number 25 on pink parchment paper, and a demonic principality of Persia with a lion's mane and gladiator sandals aggressively gripping the land. Brandon predicts a major revival breaking out across multiple cities in Brazil. In the realm of domestic politics, he states there will be a significant breakthrough in Minnesota midterms to unseat Ilhan Omar, visualizing her surrounded by a swarm of bumblebees. He mentions major decisions resting on three Supreme Court judges around February 3rd and foresees an attack on the finances of Mike Lindell, whom he visualizes with a heavier face and receding hair. Finally, Brandon stresses the importance of political engagement leading into 2026, referencing Texas redistricting and upcoming elections in Israel.
rawData: [December 12, 2025]-[The Lord Showed Me Venezuela Burning Its Oil Fields Like Iraq - Vision During Prayer]-[https://youtu.be/EuT30KAAFvY]-[Well, good evening. Thank you for joining us to look at the Christmas lights; I thought that was kind of fun. Paisley's been asking—we drive by and she says, 'Daddy, I want to go look at the lights.' I said, 'Okay, let's go look at the lights.' So, I thought, why not bring you all with us? Hopefully, you had a little fun. I know it was kind of quick, but if you don't move quick, sometimes you've just got a little bit of time left walking around. Megan was ready to go, so I thought I'd bring y'all along. When you go to the Oklahoma City Zoo Lights, they're the best; it is phenomenal. They have a huge display at the Oklahoma City Zoo, and we'll go over there and look at the lights sometimes and drive through their park. They've closed off roads where you can see the animals; they're making noises as you're looking at the lights, and it's pretty cool. It's probably one of my top favorite things to do, but it's pretty expensive. I think it's about $60 to go, and it only lasts about an hour, so you burn that money pretty quick. The hot chocolate's expensive, too, but it's something fun to do every once in a while. That's what we did tonight. I thought we'd come on and pray. Diana and I have been listening to Joyce again, and she's been talking about pride and things the Lord shapes when you're on that potter's wheel and the Lord's telling you, 'I want you to study this. I want you to keep going. I want you to come deeper with Me.' As I keep telling you all, the Lord said that in 2026, He's drawing the bride closer to His side. There are great days of shakings and awakenings coming, and things the Lord is about to do with the body of Christ in 2026. 2025 was for warning, but the manifestations of what He's told me are coming in 2026 will be a wake-up call—a wake-up call concerning the signs of the times of the coming of the Lord Jesus Christ. I say it over and over again. The Lord teaches me a lot about the love walk when people hurt or wound you, and you have to be quick to respond. I've said, 'Lord, what do you want me to do about this? How do you want me to handle this? I'm really ticked off.' And He says, 'Brandon, walk in love. Love never fails.' I say, 'They're wrong, Lord. This is wrong. I'm upset about this.' But love takes no account of a suffered wrong. Remember that. If you want to walk higher with the fire, you must learn to walk in love, even when people do you wrong. The first reaction is always where they're really at. Is it a quick repentance? Is it an excuse for how it happened? Is it 'I'm sorry,' or is it quick? That's why you can pinpoint people. But the Lord tells me that regardless of how people act or what they say, you are required to walk in love, for that's where the greater glory is. Remember what the angel said when he visited the other night? The angel with the opal armor. The glory of the Lord filled my home so strongly. It lasted for over a day after he visited. I've said before, 'I don't talk to angels,' but the angel had a word. He spoke: 'The Lord has sent me to give you a message. You want to know the key to the greater glory? You want to know how you're going to walk in the manifestations in these last days, how you'll be a frontrunner instead of the one tagging behind?' We say, 'Well, that's pride.' No, there are going to be trailblazers and there are going to be those following behind. I want to be walking with the King. When He's coming back and we're riding with Him, I want my horse to be on the front row. I want to hear Him say, 'Brandon, well done, son.' No matter how much people hurt or offend you, or how much they wound you in life—whether they are close to you or not—words hurt. People hurt people. It's not always intentional; sometimes people do it ignorantly. But I'm telling you, your first response must always be love. Sometimes you have to take a step back. In my own situations, I've noticed I sometimes freak out and say things. When I get wounded, I have a tendency to explode, and the Lord says, 'Draw back.' It's like a horse you pull back. What's your first reaction? Mine is usually anger, and I'll say a lot with my mouth, but the Lord says, 'Draw back. Love never fails, Brandon. Your job is to always walk in love.' When that angel visited and said, 'What is going to bring in the greater glory is a love foundation—your love walk. Where are you with your love?' It's the second time I've had angels talk to me like that. One was a couple of years ago on Valentine's Day. It was amazing. A lot of people say, 'I don't go with all that,' but it's still about love. Love never fails; God is love. The Lord said to me then that the thing he needs to know the most—what he'll be judged on the most—is: how did he treat his brethren? How did he walk in love? How do you walk in love toward your wife, your husband, and your kids? When people wound you, you don't have to allow them to keep hurting you, but your reaction to them is love. Now, trust is earned; I want you to understand that. It doesn't mean you trust everybody or let everyone into your circle. In fact, the first thing the Lord told me when I started this ministry was, 'Trust no one. Don't allow people in your inner circle, Brandon. There are things I will call you to do and say where you're not allowed to have friends. You'll be separated unto Me to say the hard things. There will be a target on you in the natural, and you will be required to take communion and fellowship with Me, and walk in love with your wife and kids.' I've learned even with family it's not always the easiest thing to do because they're not always going to 'get' you, and it's not required for them to get you. You are obedient unto God. The Lord even says to me regarding the emails we get, 'Fear God, not man. Fear Me, not man. Don't worry about what they're saying about you. Draw close to My side and walk with Me in this end-time harvest. Walk with Me and I'll use you greatly and mightily.' They won't understand why you're accelerating so quickly, but it's not for them. Keep humble. Keep your flesh under and keep the love walk straight. I had an opportunity this weekend to be really ticked off at some things. I'm telling you, I haven't been that mad in a long time, to the point where I was trembling. I asked, 'Lord, what do you want me to do about all this mess?' He said, "Walk in love." I said, "Are you telling me to walk in love? I'm going to go off. What do you want me to do, Lord?" "Walk in love." I said, "Walk in love." The key to the greater glory, son, is even whenever you feel somebody has done you wrong—and they did—is to walk in love. Regardless of the excuses they try to make afterwards, wrong is wrong and right is right. It doesn't matter what their response is; your response is love. And that's what he said to me. And I said, "That's hard for my flesh to hear." I'm screaming inside, you know, and it's like that. Diana is upset. D is crying. And what do you do whenever you're wounded so deep by people that you love and trust? You walk in love. And that's why the Lord said, "It doesn't mean you have to keep planted there. It doesn't mean you have to keep doing these things. It's your choice. It's your choice if you want to walk in the greater glory. I will use you mightily. But God is love. It's not what he does; it's who he is." And if you want to walk by his side, even whenever it's something innocent or not innocent, it doesn't matter. I'm not looking at them; I'm looking at him. I'm staying vertical. And you must learn to stay vertical in these days ahead. The Lord showed me as I was walking even at Rhema tonight, he said, "You must learn to stay vertical. It's not always going to be easy because the devil wants you to go horizontal and look at everybody else and what they're doing." But what about them? How come them? And he said, "No, stay vertical. Who cares about what they're doing? Your job is to be the branch and stay on the vine. Your job is to keep looking at me." Because if you look at what's around you, your pride sets in and you start thinking, "Woe is me." You start singing a sad song, and everybody has a sad song they can sing about how people do you wrong or how people might have not done it intentionally or whatever. It doesn't matter. Your job is to always maintain love. Now, love doesn't mean you have to keep doing the same thing over and over again and allowing people to hurt you. The lesson is: don't allow people in your inner circle. I've learned a lot, and I'm trying to teach you all something because Brandon and I have learned a lot as we walk down the road. This has not been an easy journey for me, but I'm learning I want to walk with him. I always know when I've messed up because that anointing lifts off me. But he said, "Be angry and sin not." He said that because I don't have much of a filter sometimes when people tick me off and I just go, and that's the way he said, "Be angry and sin not." It's okay to be angry, but don't sin in your anger. Does that make sense what I'm telling y'all? So that's the lesson I've been learning in the last 48 hours: Be angry, sin not, walk in love. You must learn to walk in love; it's the only way to the greater glory. Remember the angel. He says it's always those life lessons; that's how we're going to walk with him. Even whenever people talk trash about you or run you down, your job is always to stay on the love side. Remember that because you're going to be tested just like Brandon is tested. But hopefully, you can learn from our mistakes. So be very careful who you let in your inner courts. Be careful who you let in your inner tent. Be careful who's influencing you. Watch who's speaking into your life. Watch where you're feeding. Make sure you're growing and you're going from glory to glory. It's your responsibility to monitor your thermostat. If your thermostat is cold, check your thermostat spiritually. You say, "I'm going to a church that doesn't feed me. I'm hanging around people who pull me down." Check your thermostat. There are many of you that say, "Well, I'm married into an unequally yoked marriage, Brandon. What should I do?" Well, you shouldn't have done that in the first place, but now that you're in it, you don't get a divorce. You're in covenant. So you walk it out in love and you pray for your spouse. The Bible says, "Don't be unequally yoked with unbelievers." It's your choice. Now, if you got saved during that marriage, you keep interceding. You fast, you pray, you anoint. I would take olive oil—this is what I got from Billy Burke—and I'm telling you what, solid people, Billy Burke. You anoint their pillow with oil. You anoint them at night, and they don't even have to know you're doing it. Maybe they wake up in the morning; you put a little bit on that pillow and you anoint it. And I pray over them over and over and over again. Make your prayer petition for 2026. That's what I tried to teach you all last night. We always write the vision down of everything we're believing for every single year. Whether it's a house, we write it down. It may be something so big that you know that God is the only one that can bring it to pass. And you go, "How is that even possible? This is not possible by man's standard, but it's possible through God." So you have to write your vision down. Put it down before you. Put your love scriptures, put whatever you've got. Write the vision down. Make sure you keep that in priority and put it on a mirror, on your car visor, or by your bed where you sleep on your nightstand. Make sure you have that as something that's always in front of your face. You have to keep the vision in front of you. That's why I try to tell people all the time: if you don't have big house mentality, go drive in the big neighborhoods and get that inside of you so that you can start thinking bigger. Poverty thinks poverty; rich thinks rich. Does that make sense? If you think like a winner, you don't go around a losing baseball team or a losing football team and get winning inside of you. If they're always losing, loser is going to be inside of you. You've got to get around a winner. Go watch somebody who's a champion. You've got to get that inside of you so that it changes you from the inside out. Add the Word to it, but get a vision. Get an inward vision of who you are in Christ and know that God's created you to be more than a conqueror. You are not made to be a 'just barely getting by' person. And maybe you're almost at the championship game and then you lose. Well, you learn from the losing and you get better the next year. There is a place in God that the devil does not go when you're underneath that umbrella of the anointing. You must learn to abide in love and abide in the glory of the Lord, even whenever your flesh does not want to. There are days whenever I just go, "Oh!" Like I said, I want to pull my hair out, and it's already pulled out. I'm trying to tell you all it's something Brandon's walking through, too. But I so want that when I get on the other side, I say, "Jesus, I did everything I could. I sure tried." I don't want to say, "Well, I didn't leave it all on the table. I wasn't all in." I think a lot of people might have regrets, ladies and gentlemen, but it's what you do with the time and the effort that you've been given. I came across a guy that was really ripped. He had muscles on muscles, and it challenges you because you look at him and go, "Wow, he has the same hours in a day as I have, but he's really trim and fit and he looks good, and then here I am." But where are you spiritually? I try to mirror myself off of Jesus, but at the same time, I'm going, "Where are people spiritually?" Where are people physically? I know I need to do better with my flesh, but I'm not satisfied with where I am spiritually either. I want more. I want to stand with Him. And the only way you're going to do that is to act like Jesus and be more like Him. And what did Jesus do, even whenever they were spitting on Him? Sometimes the best answer is no answer at all. Sometimes walking in love—whenever people are wanting a comment from you, wanting you to respond—sometimes to walk in love is not to give an answer at all. Just be still. Jesus didn't respond to it; He got quiet before them. They are the accusers of the brethren, the accusers of the palace. 'Hey, what do you say to this?' Silence. 'They're accusing you. They want to put you to death.' Silence. So, His example is that not everything requires a reaction. I'm learning that too. Sometimes no words is a good thing. We're all a work in progress. We're all learning. But I want to teach you all as much as what I'm learning, especially today and yesterday. Walk in love. Walk in love. Love never fails. You either have it or you don't. Either you're a believing believer or you're not. That was 17 minutes—that's not too bad for that part. I could have probably said more, but I try to save your time. And even reading Diana—she's a firecracker, too. But I tell you, listening... because the Lord told us, because we're talking about pride and Leviathan and the pride behind it and the spirits that try to be sneaky and you think you're humble. But you know what? This is something else. People say, 'Braden, you're humble.' 'Boy, you're humble, Brandon.' People say that all the time. Either you are or you're not. There's no pretending with humility. It's displayed by a reaction of love when you realize that it's not you doing something. You don't have to convince yourself. Either you are or you're not. I don't have to walk around and try to display humility. Either it is or it's not. 'Well, Brandon, you're humble. Brandon, you're prideful. Brandon, this whatever.' Either you are or you're not. You cannot fake humility. Either it's you, either it's Jesus Christ in you, or it's not. But I don't have to vocalize it. That's why I don't ever say I try to be whatever, because I know. If you look at my resume, you know Brandon doesn't have what it takes in the natural. Do you understand? Brandon is solely dependent upon Jesus. I don't have much education. People say, 'Brandon, don't knock yourself.' No, I'm stating a fact. That's not me trying to be humble; that's just stating a fact. I am flat-out nothing without Jesus Christ or my education. When you can barely read and write... things that I do read well, I've read probably a thousand times or I've studied it. If it's raw, like reading those articles that Braden puts up, you can take it to the bank: I'm going to be embarrassed. But I learned... even whenever I played games at Christmas time with people at youth parties, I would be the one hiding in the back because I was so embarrassed to play those games because they had to read and I couldn't read. I remember sitting in there and they would play those games at Christmas time and they said, 'Braden, do you want to play?' I said, 'Oh, no. I'm not interested,' and I'd make up an excuse. But really, the bottom line is I wanted to play with all my heart. I wanted to play with all my heart, but I was so ashamed that I was a teenager and I couldn't read that I didn't want to embarrass myself in front of all the high school kids. So, I would go and do something else and talk to the adults. A lot of times I'd go in and talk the Word to the mother of the party or the father because I was so ashamed that I couldn't do what the other kids were doing. That's what I developed; it was a defense mechanism. A lot of that came out of just a lot of pain. And even whenever I was a janitor and cleaning for all these years, it was the mechanism of rejection. There was so much rejection going through a lot of pastors. You know, it's like with us—nobody really cares truly about you. If I were still the janitor, nobody would give a crap. But because I have a million people following me now, people try to reach out to you. But really, the bottom line is, if you were still the janitor, they wouldn't even look your direction. They don't give a crap about you. Remember that. It's about Jesus inside of you. And when He exalts you and promotes you, people look your way. I was the janitor all the time. I went there and I cleaned 90 toilets two or three times a week. And even then, they said, 'Go in there and clean your bathrooms. You aren't going to touch nobody and lay hands on anybody.' But the glory was on my hands even then. And I could lay hands on people and the power of God would hit them and cancer would fall off their face. I'll never forget this lady walked up to me while I was vacuuming the floor. Her boy was diagnosed with stage four cancer and she came up to me while I was cleaning the church and she said, 'I've heard... we're desperate. There's nobody in this church that knows how to pray for anybody, but could you please pray for my son? He starts chemo in a couple of days. Can you lay hands on him? Do you see this knot on the side of his face? It's the size of an egg. Can you pray for it?' I said, 'Yes.' So, I shut the door because I didn't want to get in trouble. I opened the door and I said, 'Come on in here, buddy.' I said, 'In the name of Jesus Christ, I cursed that cancer and told it to go.' Three days later, she came back to the church crying. She said, 'Brandon, look, it's gone.' Not by what Brandon did, but by what Jesus did through Brandon. And when you realize 'Christ in me, Christ in me,' that little boy's life—he didn't have to go to chemo. They went back to the doctor. They went back to the doctor and the doctor said, 'What happened?' She told him and he tried to make up an excuse that it wasn't cancer. I thought it was funny. I said, 'Isn't Jesus wonderful?' Do you understand? Jesus will meet you where you are and He loves you. But when you're going through the valley with all this pressure, like I'm learning, you're not important until you have a following in the world's eyes. But Jesus saw you as important when you were scrubbing poop. Do you understand? And so I don't get into all of the political stuff even when I'm invited to the White House. It's all a show pony. I refuse to be show ponies because you weren't there when I was a janitor and you didn't care then. So I realize the bigger the platform, the bigger the call what God has put inside of me. And regardless of how much God promotes Brandon Biggs, He always says, 'Stay the janitor, Brandon. Don't ever forget where I brought you from.' Never forget it, for you're about to enter into the second phase of your ministry and healing is going to fall out of your hands and cancer is going to dry up on a greater scale. The fire is going to drive out sickness like you wouldn't believe, son. But that's going to be the time whenever your ministry really takes off. Remember this and always keep small in your own eyes. I was out doing my yard work and He always tells me that: 'Remember to stay small in your own eyes.' And it was a flattering thing even this week. I was sent the videos of the Christmas party tonight with President Trump. And I got to see Billy Graham—the son spoke. I got to see another man. I don't know who he was, Benny Johnson. These other people were there. They all went to the Christmas party tonight, the one that I said I didn't want to go to. I was very excited that they sent me the videos and I got to hear what President Trump said to the people, saying we're going to say Christmas again as a nation. He said a lot of things that were really cool about the Christians and was saying that we're bringing religion back or Christianity back and the things that he was saying from the platform, talking to all those pastors who were invited to come to the Christmas party tonight. So, I thought it was really neat that they shared with me what was said. I was excited that they included me even after I rejected the invitation. But I thought, you know what? Jesus is wonderful. Even when they say, "Hey, next time you will be here. Next time we want you here. Next time you will come to these parties. We want you here." I take it as whatever, you know? All I want is just to sit on my little couch here and to teach you all how to pray. If you don't understand the authority of the believer, if you don't understand who you are, you're not going to make it in the days ahead. I say this not to scare you, but to prepare you. You must understand Psalms 91. You must understand Psalms 23. You must understand the authority of the believer. I always try to talk about Sister Billy. She's been such a friend. I want you to understand you must understand the authority of the believer, and you read these little books like this and you get that authority down inside you. I didn't realize how many people have no idea about who they are in Christ and you must get this down inside of you. You must, and she would even tell you to walk in love. So always stay small as much as God promotes you. And there'll be a lot of you that you've been the janitor just like Brandon Biggs. And you'll look back at your life and you'll pinch yourself at what God's about to do because all to Jesus we surrender. All to him I humbly give. Do you understand? Diana was clerical. I entered in all the offerings. I counted the offerings. I did it all for years, counting the offerings and entering in your tithe and offerings at the megachurch we worked at. And we learned a lot. But never turn your nose up to small beginnings. So because one day maybe they'll call you to want to go to the White House and maybe you will say no, maybe you'll say yes, but it's all about him. You know, all the conferences that I've been invited to to speak to tens of thousands of people on a regular basis, I'm going to say, well, it has been all of them, haven't it? All of them I have said no to. It's not funny. I just don't, I'm not into it. It's just real. And I had to say to myself, you know, well, I have to say I spoke at Sister Billy's tent thing. Yeah, and I like that. That was fun. But it wasn't on the internet, so I felt good about it. You don't have to worry about it because what if you mess up? What if you say something really stupid and it's on there and you're not in control of the video? But to me, it's just like whatever. So, all 4,040 of you, you got my little lesson today about walking in love. I was going to bring in Diana. So, if she wants to pray with us, I told her I'll talk for a second. Oh, 4,040. Here, move that camera up a little bit. Let me get her. I want to see if she can pray because I talk and then I let her come in because she's really busy. You all keep her busy. She is busy doing the emails, man. We're a three-man army. Braden does the editing and all the stuff. Diana does the emails and talking to the people and getting all that done, sending out the Bibles, and then Brandon tries to keep himself full and talking to you all. And so it's pretty crazy, but all right. Hold on. Who I was going to ask this question, I don't know if you all, I tried to do this poll. A lot of these people don't know who Kathryn Kuhlman is. So I did a poll last time. I think like 30% didn't know who she was, which is amazing to me. I think it was like 30 to 40%. So, it was pretty high. Hi. She probably is cooking right now. I don't know. Here, let me see. I was going to say, do you know who Billy Graham was? Because I know a lot of people who don't know. I don't know how you couldn't know. But okay, she's here. What the heck? Hi. I brought my friend here. I was just telling them about everything and walking in love and all that. They enjoy the Christmas lights. I don't know. I hope so. You know, we don't do that very often, but it's something fun. You want to take communion with me? Mhm. You can pray with me. Jesus, we sure love you, and we thank you, Father, for the days ahead. No matter how hard it is or how easy it is, we always give Jesus the glory. And we always keep him first. It's not always easy, but your flesh wants to be rebellious. It wants to act out, don't it? But God has made a way. And it's when you stay in the word, you got to get in the word. Father, we thank you for the body. We thank you, Father, for all these prayer warriors tonight, all 4,240 of them. Every person here tonight, Father, are so hungry and so desperate. It's in the middle of the night and they're here. Lord, I'm thankful, Father, for every person that's here tonight. I'm thankful for this congregation of people. Lord, we've even prayed over if we open up our own thing and have a building and open up our own little church and pray with the people more like that. We've been praying about it. I don't know if that's real wisdom for me because of people not liking me too much, but it's something that I have to really pray over. I'm not the most liked by some groups of people. So, it's not like having our pastors or whatever. It's not having a church like them. People don't hate them like they hate us. And it's not funny. People have developed an opinion about us, really. And there's a lot of you that love us and would go, but I'd have to have hired security continually. Armed security guards, armed police officers. I'd have to have several to have a meeting just to make sure the congregation was safe and Diana and I would remain safe. And so it's really something I'd have to really pray about because Diana and I talked about it last night and we would do it if he asked us to. Yeah, it's been on my heart because I like to pray live. It's amazing because there's a bigger pool, you know, whenever you're with a group of people and you can feel them. But it's putting yourself out there that you have security already and making sure they're armed and that there's no more incidences like Charlie Kirk, you know, and I don't want anybody to be in harm's way because of me and having a meeting. So, I would have to hire police officers or security. So, there's a lot more to us opening a building and having something in Tulsa. I would put it by ORU because of the college kids. I have a hunger. And so, there's just things that I have to pray about a lot. And security and being safe is a major deal for us, you know, because we have a lot of people that just don't like Brandon and Diana. And that's fine. But, Father, we thank you, Lord. We thank you, Lord. I had to say that as we're praying over that. And I, Father, I thank you, Lord, for your body. We thank you, Lord, for how much you love us and how much you've taken care of us.
